name: East Alabama Community
description_short: East Alabama Community Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution established in 1958, serving over 3,200 members in Opelika, Alabama with personal banking, lending, and deposit products.
description_long:
East Alabama Community Federal Credit Union was established in 1958 and is headquartered in Opelika, Alabama. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution designed to serve a specific geographic community. The credit union has stewardship of $20.4 million in assets and maintains a modest operational footprint with one branch location and one ATM.

The institution offers a comprehensive range of personal banking services including checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs). On the lending side, EACFCU provides auto loans (new and used vehicles), personal loans, credit cards, and mortgage options. Additional services include online and mobile banking, bill pay functionality, e-statements, wire transfers, drive-thru teller access, and online loan payments. The credit union also provides financial calculators and routing number information to support member banking needs.

East Alabama Community distinguishes itself as a locally-rooted institution with deep community ties spanning over 65 years of operation. The credit union serves a defined membership base of 3,200+ members with a lean staff of over 5 dedicated employees, suggesting personalized service and local decision-making. The institution's asset size and membership base position it as a genuine community financial cooperative rather than a large commercial operation.

As a small credit union, EACFCU offers authentic community banking with member-focused governance and local lending decisions. However, prospective members should note the limited branch network (one location), relatively modest asset base compared to larger institutions, and the fact that current website information indicates both lobby and drive-thru hours are currently closed. The institution is best suited for individuals who live or work in the Opelika area and value community-based financial relationships over branch convenience. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Financial Calculators (Mortgage, Auto Loan, Credit Card Payoff)
pros: Member-owned credit union structure prioritizes member benefits over profits
Established institution with 65+ years of operational history and community trust
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, auto loans, personal loans, credit cards, and mortgages
Mobile app available for both iOS and Android devices for convenient banking access
Online banking with bill pay and e-statements for digital account management
Serves specific geographic community with deep local ties and local decision-making
Financial calculators provided for mortgage, auto loan, and credit card planning
cons: Only one physical branch location limits accessibility for members outside immediate Opelika area
Current website indicates both lobby and drive-thru hours are closed, creating service uncertainty
Small asset base ($20.4 million) may limit loan approval amounts and product innovation compared to larger institutions
Limited ATM network (1 ATM) may inconvenience members needing cash access outside branch location
No customer reviews available on CreditDoc, making it difficult to assess member satisfaction and service quality
best_for: Opelika and surrounding East Alabama residents seeking community-focused banking relationships
Members who prioritize credit union member-ownership governance over large bank operations
Small business owners and local individuals needing personalized lending decisions
Community members valuing long-term institutional stability and local financial stewardship
diagnosis: East Alabama Community FCU is best for individuals living or working in the Opelika, Alabama area who want genuine community banking with member-ownership benefits. The primary caveat is the single-branch limitation and current service hour closures, which may restrict accessibility, and the institution's small asset base may limit product sophistication or loan approval amounts compared to larger regional credit unions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
